question: Who became the youngest nominee in Grammy Awards history at the 66th ceremony?
source_sentence: Monét's 2-year-old daughter, Hazel, became the youngest nominee in Grammy Awards history; she was a featured artist on her mother's song "Hollywood", which was nominated for Best Traditional R&B Performance.

question: Who won the inaugural Best African Performance award at the 66th Grammy Awards?
source_sentence: South African singer Tyla was the winner of the inaugural Best African Performance award.

question: Which band became the first female-fronted rock band to win the Grammy Award for Best Rock Album?
source_sentence: Paramore became the first female-fronted rock band to win the Grammy Award for Best Rock Album.

question: How many total categories were there at the 66th Annual Grammy Awards?
source_sentence: Three new categories – Best African Music Performance, Best Alternative Jazz Album and Best Pop Dance Recording – were added. There are 94 categories total.

question: What album did Taylor Swift announce while accepting her Best Pop Vocal Album award at the 66th Grammys?
source_sentence: Swift announced the release of her album The Tortured Poets Department while accepting the award for Best Pop Vocal Album.

question: Who hosted the Premiere ceremony at the 66th Annual Grammy Awards?
source_sentence: Justin Tranter was announced as the host for the Premiere ceremony on January 25, 2024, alongside the list of presenters.

question: Which engineer extended his record with a fifth Album of the Year-category award at the 66th Grammys?
source_sentence: Engineer Serban Ghenea extended his record with a fifth award in the category as well.

question: Which artist performed at the Grammys from their residency at Sphere in Las Vegas?
source_sentence: U2 joined the lineup on January 27, with a performance from their U2:UV residency at Sphere in Las Vegas.

question: Who received the MusiCares Person of the Year award at the 66th Grammy Awards?
source_sentence: Jon Bon Jovi

question: Which IBM generative AI tool was used to generate editorial content around nominees at the 66th Grammy Awards?
source_sentence: Watsonx, a generative AI tool of IBM, was used to generate editorial content around nominees during the presentation.

question: Which song tied with 'Up, Up and Away' to become the joint-most decorated song in Grammy history?
source_sentence: Kendrick Lamar's "Not Like Us" swept all five of its nominations, which included Record of the Year and Song of the Year, tying with "Up, Up and Away" to become the joint-most decorated song in Grammy Awards history.

question: Who won Best New Artist at the 67th Annual Grammy Awards?
source_sentence: Chappell Roan took home Best New Artist, and Sierra Ferrell swept the American roots categories, winning all four of her nominations.

question: Who swept the American roots categories at the 67th Grammys, winning all four nominations?
source_sentence: Chappell Roan took home Best New Artist, and Sierra Ferrell swept the American roots categories, winning all four of her nominations.

question: What was the title of the Best Rap Album winner by Doechii at the 67th Grammy Awards?
source_sentence: Best New Artist nominee Doechii won Best Rap Album for Alligator Bites Never Heal, becoming the third woman to win the award after Hill (with the Fugees) in 1997 and Cardi B in 2019.

question: Which small business was featured with Doja Cat in a wildfire-relief commercial during the 67th Grammy Awards broadcast?
source_sentence: Featured businesses included Orla Floral Studio of Altadena with an appearance by Doja Cat, Two Dragons Martial Arts of Altadena with an appearance by Charlie Puth, Rhythms Of The Village of Altadena with an appearance by Anderson .Paak, Paliskates Skate Shop in Pacific Palisades with an appearance by Avril Lavigne, and the L.A. Lost Stuffy Project with an appearance by the Jonas Brothers.

question: Which Beatles song became the first AI-assisted production to be nominated for a Grammy?
source_sentence: "Now and Then" by the Beatles is the first song produced with the help of artificial intelligence to be nominated for a Grammy.

question: What was Beyoncé's career total of Grammy nominations after the 67th ceremony announcements?
source_sentence: With a career total of 99 nominations, Beyoncé became the most nominated artist in Grammy history, breaking a tie with her husband Jay-Z.

question: Which album by Anitta became the first funk carioca album nominated for a Grammy?
source_sentence: Anitta's Funk Generation is the first funk carioca album to be nominated for a Grammy.

question: Who received the Salute To Industry Icons honor at the 67th Grammy Awards?
source_sentence: Jody Gerson

question: Which band was named MusiCares Persons of the Year for the 67th Grammy Awards?
source_sentence: Grateful Dead

question: Which American psychedelic rock keyboardist and Iron Butterfly member died in May 2024?
source_sentence: 24 – Doug Ingle, 78, American psychedelic rock singer-songwriter and keyboardist (Iron Butterfly)

question: At what age did Glynis Johns, the British musical theatre actress and singer, die in January 2024?
source_sentence: Glynis Johns, 100, British musical theatre actress and singer

question: Which former One Direction member died in October 2024 at the age of 31?
source_sentence: Liam Payne, 31, English pop singer (One Direction)

question: Which Grateful Dead bassist died in October 2024?
source_sentence: 25 – Phil Lesh, 84, American psychedelic rock singer-songwriter and bassist (Grateful Dead)

question: Which American record producer, songwriter, arranger, and jazz trumpeter died on November 3, 2024 at age 91?
source_sentence: Quincy Jones, 91, American record producer, songwriter, arranger, and jazz trumpeter

question: Which South Korean record producer died in February 2024 at the age of 40?
source_sentence: Shinsadong Tiger, 40, South Korean record producer

question: Which American post-hardcore musician and record producer, known for Big Black and Shellac, died in May 2024?
source_sentence: Steve Albini, 61, American post-hardcore and noise rock singer and guitarist (Big Black, Shellac) and record producer

question: Which French pop and yé-yé singer-songwriter died on June 11, 2024?
source_sentence: Françoise Hardy, 80, French pop and yé-yé singer-songwriter

question: Which Indian classical and jazz fusion tabla player died on December 15, 2024?
source_sentence: Zakir Hussain, 73, Indian classical and jazz fusion tabla player (Shakti)

question: Which My Chemical Romance drummer was found dead in November 2024 at the age of 44?
source_sentence: Bob Bryar, 44, American alternative rock drummer (My Chemical Romance) (body discovered on this date)

question: Which film surpassed The Lion King to become the highest-grossing animated film of all time in 2024?
source_sentence: Inside Out 2 surpassed The Lion King to become the highest-grossing animated film of all time and became the 8th highest grossing film of all time upon its release.

question: Which film became the highest-grossing unrated film of all time in 2024?
source_sentence: Terrifier 3 became the highest-grossing unrated film of all time.

question: Who directed the Palme d'Or winner at the 77th Cannes Film Festival?
source_sentence: Anora, directed by Sean Baker, United States

question: What film won the Golden Lion at the 81st Venice International Film Festival?
source_sentence: The Room Next Door, directed by Pedro Almodóvar, Spain

question: What film won the People's Choice Award at the 49th Toronto International Film Festival?
source_sentence: The Life of Chuck, directed by Mike Flanagan, United States

question: Which animated film series became the first to gross $5 billion with a 2024 release?
source_sentence: The Despicable Me film franchise became the first animated film series to gross $5 billion with the release of Despicable Me 4.

question: Which film surpassed Mamma Mia! as the highest-grossing Broadway musical film adaptation of all time?
source_sentence: It surpassed Mamma Mia! (2008) as the highest-grossing Broadway musical film adaptation of all time.

question: Deadpool & Wolverine surpassed which 1990 film's record to become the highest-grossing R-rated Disney film?
source_sentence: Deadpool & Wolverine became the highest-grossing R-rated Disney film, surpassing the 34-year-old record set by Pretty Woman in 1990.

question: In which film did Blue Ivy Carter make her film debut in 2024?
source_sentence: Blue Ivy Carter – Mufasa: The Lion King

question: What film won the Golden Bear at the 74th Berlin International Film Festival?
source_sentence: Dahomey, directed by Mati Diop, France

question: Which animated film surpassed Inside Out 2 to become the highest-grossing animated film of all time in 2025?
source_sentence: It surpassed Inside Out 2 (2024) as the highest-grossing animated film, breaking Disney's 14-year streak with the title after regaining it with Toy Story 3 (2010).

question: Which studio became the first non-Hollywood studio to hold the distinction of producing the highest-grossing animated film?
source_sentence: Doing so, Enlight Pictures became the first non-Hollywood studio to hold this distinction.

question: Who directed the Palme d'Or winner at the 78th Cannes Film Festival?
source_sentence: It Was Just an Accident, directed by Jafar Panahi, Iran

question: Which film won the People's Choice Award at the 50th Toronto International Film Festival?
source_sentence: Hamnet, directed by Chloé Zhao, United Kingdom & United States

question: Which film surpassed Frozen 2 to become Walt Disney Animation Studios' highest-grossing film?
source_sentence: On 31 December, thirty-six days after release, Zootopia 2 surpassed Frozen 2 to become Walt Disney Animation Studios' highest-grossing film.

question: What was the worldwide opening weekend gross of Zootopia 2?
source_sentence: It claimed the fourth highest-grossing worldwide opening of all time ($560.3 million), placing behind Avengers: Endgame ($1.2 billion), Avengers: Infinity War ($641 million), and Spider-Man: No Way Home ($601 million).

question: Which horror film surpassed It's record for the largest opening weekend for a horror film?
source_sentence: The Conjuring: Last Rites had a $194 million opening weekend worldwide, surpassing It's record ($190 million) for the largest opening weekend for a horror film.

question: Who directed the Golden Lion winner at the 82nd Venice International Film Festival?
source_sentence: Father Mother Sister Brother, directed by Jim Jarmusch, United States

question: Which Japanese anime film became the highest-grossing Japanese film of all time in 2025?
source_sentence: Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba Infinity Castle grossed about $780 million worldwide to become the highest-grossing Japanese film of all time, surpassing its predecessor Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba Mugen Train.

question: Which live-action animated film became the first to globally gross at least $1 billion?
source_sentence: It became the highest-grossing live-action animated film, surpassing The Smurfs (2011), as well as the first live-action animated film to globally gross at least $1 billion.
